zh.lifeSummary: 當Edward Saint Barbe Esquire於大約1562年出生在英國英格蘭維爾爾特帕利申時，他的父親William Saint Barbe Privey Chamber to Henry VIII65歲，母親Mrs Elizabeth Saint Barbe51歲。他於大約1583年與Christian Beswick在英國米德萊可結婚。他們至少是8個兒子和2個女兒的父母。他去世於1642年享年81歲。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Edward Saint Barbe Esquire于大约1562年出生在英国英格兰維爾爾特帕利申时，他的父亲William Saint Barbe Privey Chamber to Henry VIII65岁，母亲Mrs Elizabeth Saint Barbe51岁。他于大约1583年与Christian Beswick在英国米德萊可结婚。他们至少是8个儿子和2个女儿的父母。他去世于1642年享年81岁。

nameMeanings.name: Barbe
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  French Emile, Laurent, Andre, Gillis, Jean Pierre, Serge, Sylvie, Thierry, Yves.
French: descriptive nickname for someone with a beard, Old French barbe (from Latin barba).
French (Barbé): adjectival derivative of 1, meaning ‘bearded’.

variants.name: Barb
meanings: German: variant of   Barbe  . This surname is also found in France (mainly Alsace).
Romanian: variant of   Barbu  .

variants.name: Barbier
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  French Dominique, Emile, Pierre, Alexandre, Gilles, Jacques, Laurent, Laurette, Manon, Marcel, Marcellin, Michel.
French: occupational name for a barber-surgeon (see Barber ), Old French barbier (from Late Latin barbarius, a derivative of barba ‘beard’). Compare Barbe and Barbee .
German: occupational name for a barber, from Middle High German barbierer, or an occupational name for an armorer, from barbier(e) ‘metal visor (to protect the face)’.

variants.name: Barbon
meanings: English: habitational name from Barbon in Westmorland. The placename is probably from Old Norse bjórr ‘beaver’ + brunnr ‘stream’, alternatively from Old English bera ‘bear’ + burna ‘stream’.
English: nickname from Middle English bare bon ‘bare bone’, perhaps denoting a thin or bare-legged man.
French and English (of French Huguenot origin): nickname for a bearded man, from an augmentative of French   Barbe  .

variants.name: Barby
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  German Merwin, Otto, Wolfgang.  French Celestine, Pierre.
German: habitational name from a place so named in Saxony-Anhalt.
French (mainly Dordogne): derivative of   Barbe   1.

variants.name: Barbin
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  French Angelle, Marcel.
French: diminutive of   Barbe   1.

variants.name: Barbot
meanings: French:
from the Old French female personal name Barbot, a diminutive of Barbe , a short form of Barbara .
nickname for someone who tended to speak indistinctly, from French barboter ‘to babble’, or for someone with a beard, from a diminutive of Old French barbe ‘beard’.

variants.name: Barbay
meanings: French: variant of Barbé (see Barbe ).

nameMeanings.name: Edward
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: From an Old English personal name derived from ēad ‘prosperity, riches’ + weard ‘guard’. This has been one of the most successful of all Old English names, in frequent use from before the Conquest to the present day, and even being exported into other European languages. It was the name of three Anglo-Saxon kings and has been borne by eight kings of England since the Norman Conquest. It is also the name of the youngest son of Queen Elizabeth II . The most influential early bearer was King Edward the Confessor ( ?1002–66 ; ruled 1042–66 ). In a troubled period of English history, he contrived to rule fairly and (for a time at any rate) firmly. But in the latter part of his reign he paid more attention to his religion than to his kingdom. He died childless, and his death sparked off conflicting claims to his throne, which were resolved by the victory of William the Conqueror at the Battle of Hastings. His memory was honoured by Normans and English alike, for his fairness and his piety. Edward's mother was Norman; he had spent part of his youth in Normandy; and William claimed to have been nominated by Edward as his successor. Edward was canonized in the 12th century, and came to be venerated throughout Europe as a model of a Christian king.

variants.name: Edoardo
meanings: From English. See Edward .

variants.name: Eudard

But in the latter part of his reign he paid more attention to his religion than to his kingdom. Edward was canonized in the 12th century, and came to be venerated throughout Europe as a model of a Christian king.

variants.name: Eduardo
meanings: From English. See Edward .

variants.name: Eideard
meanings: Gaelic equivalent of Edward .

variants.name: Édouard
meanings: From English. See Edward .

variants.name: Edvard
meanings: form of English Edward .

nameMeanings.name: Saint
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  French Andre, Cyr, Remy.

English and French: nickname for a particularly pious individual, from Old French and Middle English saint(e), seint(e) ‘holy’ (from Latin sanctus ‘blameless, holy’). The vocabulary word was occasionally used in the Middle Ages as a personal name and this may have given rise to some instances of the surname.
English: perhaps a nickname for someone who wore, made, or sold girdles, from Middle English ceint, seint, saint ‘girdle’ (Old French ceint, Latin cinctum). Compare Center .

variants.name: Center
meanings: English and Scottish (Aberdeenshire): metonymic occupational name from Middle English, Old French ceinture, seinture ‘girdle’, denoting someone who made or sold girdles, or perhaps a nickname for someone who wore a distinctive girdle.
English: variant of   Senter   1.
Americanized form of German   Zehnder   or of some other similar (like-sounding) surname.

variants.name: Sant
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Krishan, Anagha, Anjali, Arvind, Deepak, Milind, Nilima, Ravindra, Sanjeev, Sanjiv, Vasudev.
English (Cheshire and Staffordshire): variant of   Saint  .
Italian (northeastern): variant of   Santo  .

variants.name: Sante
meanings: name bestowed in honour of the medieval poet Dante Alighieri ( 1265–1321 ), also used in the English-speaking world. As a medieval given name Dante was a contracted form of Durante ‘steadfast, enduring’. In an English context it was famously borne by the pre-Raphaelite poet and painter Dante Gabriel Rossetti ( 1828–1882 ), the son of an exiled Italian scholar and patriot.

en.lifeSummary: When Edward Saint Barbe Esquire was born about 1562, in Whiteparish, Wiltshire, England, United Kingdom, his father, William Saint Barbe Privey Chamber to Henry VIII, was 65 and his mother, Mrs Elizabeth Saint Barbe, was 51. He married Christian Beswick about 1583, in Middlesex, England. They were the parents of at least 8 sons and 2 daughters. He died in 1642, at the age of 81.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Edward Saint Barbe Esquire est né environ 1562, à Whiteparish, Wiltshire, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni, son père, William Saint Barbe Privey Chamber to Henry VIII, avait 65 ans et sa mère, Mrs Elizabeth Saint Barbe, avait 51 ans. Il a épousé Christian Beswick environ 1583, à Middlesex, Angleterre. Ils ont eu au moins 8 fils et 2 filles. Il est décédé en 1642, à l’âge de 81 ans.

Er hat um 1583 in Middlesex, England Christian Beswick geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 8 Söhne und 2 Töchter. Er starb 1642 im Alter von 81 Jahren.

Lui sposato Christian Beswick circa 1583, nel Middlesex, England. Erano genitori di almeno 8 figli e 2 figlie. Morì nel 1642, all'età di 81 anni.

ja.lifeSummary: Edward Saint Barbe Esquireが約1562年にイギリスイギリスウィルトシュアイアウィテパーイッシュで生まれた時，父William Saint Barbe Privey Chamber to Henry VIIIは65歳，母Mrs Elizabeth Saint Barbeは51歳であった。約1583年にイギリスミッドレセックスでChristian Beswickと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも8人の息子と2人の娘の親であった。彼は1642年に81歳で死去した。

ko.Middlesex, Accomack, Virginia, British C: 브리티시 콜론이앨 어머릭아 버지니아 주 악코맥 미들레세스
ko.lifeSummary: Edward Saint Barbe Esquire(이)가 대략 1562년 영국 잉글랜드 윌츠히르 휘테파리시에서 태어났을 때, 그의 아버지 William Saint Barbe Privey Chamber to Henry VIII(은)는 65세였고, 그의 어머니 Mrs Elizabeth Saint Barbe(은)는 51세였다. 그는 대략 1583년 영국 미들레세스에서 Christian Beswick(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 여덟 명의 아들과 두 명의 딸을 두었다. 그는 1642년 81세의 나이로 사망했다.

ru.Middlesex, Accomack, Virginia, British C: миддлесекс, аккомакк, Виргиния, бритиш колоняль амирика
ru.lifeSummary: Когда Edward Saint Barbe Esquire родился около 1562 г. в местности уитепэриш, уилцхир, Англия, Великобритания, его отцу по имени William Saint Barbe Privey Chamber to Henry VIII было 65 лет, а матери, которую звали Mrs Elizabeth Saint Barbe, 51. Он женился на женщине по имени Christian Beswick около 1583 г. в местности миддлесекс, Англия. У них было по крайней мере 8 сыновей и 2 дочери. Он умер в 1642 г. в возрасте 81 год.

Se casó con Christian Beswick aproximadamente 1583, en Middlesex, Inglaterra. Fueron padres de por lo menos 8 hijos y 2 hijas. Murió en 1642, a la edad de 81 años.
